description: Frozen held-out scenario suite for the Paper-WorkFlow SkillOpt-style improvement loop. Each scenario is a research-task archetype that exercises a documented routing path and design gate. The scorer in score_skill.py measures whether the skill DOCUMENT satisfies its own routing / gate / context / reproducibility / user-burden contracts on these scenarios. Splits mirror SkillOpt: train scenarios may inspire edits; the selection split gates acceptance; the regression split guards against silent breakage. Do not move a scenario between splits to make a number look better.

split_policy.train: May be used to motivate a proposed edit. Never gate an edit on train scenarios alone.
split_policy.selection: Held-out. The mean selection-split total score is the number a candidate edit must strictly beat.
split_policy.regression: Held-out. Must not drop versus baseline; protects designs not targeted by the current edit.

scenarios.prompt: Staggered treatment rollout across U.S. states; user wants an event-study with a clean parallel-trends check and a heterogeneity-robust ATT (not naive TWFE).
Endogenous regressor with a plausibly exogenous instrument; user needs first-stage strength, exclusion narrative, and weak-IV-robust inference.
Sharp regression discontinuity around an eligibility cutoff; user needs bandwidth selection, robust bias-corrected CIs, and a density manipulation test.
Single treated unit with a long pre-period and a donor pool; user wants synthetic control with placebo/leave-one-out inference, possibly SDID.
Observational panel with high-dimensional fixed effects; user needs HDFE OLS with cluster-robust (and small-cluster-aware) inference.
Heterogeneous treatment effects with many covariates; user wants a causal forest / DML estimate with cross-fitting and nuisance diagnostics.
Macro time-series with several variables; user wants a VAR with impulse responses and a cointegration check.
Average treatment effect with high-dimensional controls and possible model misspecification; user wants orthogonal-score DML with sensitivity analysis.
User has a DAG and wants identification by backdoor/front-door plus refutation tests for an observational design.
